metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_980
metadata.comment:
Rationale: The paper characterizes a postsynaptic signal transduction pathway, where a CaMKK activates CaMKIalpha, which then binds to and phosphorylates βPIX. The signal transduction pathway likely operates during synaptogenesis.

Figure 2D shows that CaMKIalpha phosphorylates Ser516 in βPIX.

Figure 5A shows that the overexpression of a dominant-negative CaMKIalpha or of a phosphorylation-deficient βPIX reduce the spine-density.
Figure 5D demonstrates an effect of the siRNA-mediated knock-down of CaMKIalpha on spinogenesis, whereas the knock-down of CaMKIbeta has no effect.. Experimental description: For figure 2: No information whether the cDNA used were from human of rat (also in supplemental file)
For Figure 5: 'Medium-density (3.2 × 104 cells per square centimeter) hippocampal neurons were isolated from P0-2 Sprague Dawley rat as described (Wayman et al., 2006).'
